# Postman Performance Test

A powerful Node.js library for running performance tests on Postman collections with advanced load testing patterns.

## 🚀 Features

- **Multiple Load Patterns**: Fixed, Ramp-up, Spike, Peak and Custom load profiles
- **Postman Collection Support**: Native support for Postman collections and environments
- **Data File Integration**: Support for CSV/JSON data files with random/sequential distribution
- **Pause/Resume Control**: Runtime control over test execution

## 📦 Installation

```bash
npm install @postman/performance-test
```

## 🏃‍♂️ Quick Start

### Basic Usage

```javascript
const fs = require('fs');
const Perftest = require('@postman/performance-test');

async function runTest() {
    const optionsPerf = {
        collection: JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('./assets/collection.json')),
        durationMin: 1,
        vuCount: 50,
        runId: 'your-run-id'
    };

    const test = await new Perftest().create(optionsPerf);
    
    if (!test) {
        console.error('Failed to create test');
        return;
    }

    // Event listeners
    test.on('init', () => console.log('Test initialized'));
    test.on('start', () => console.log('Test started'));


    // Start the test
    test.start((error) => {
        // run completed
        if (error) {
            console.error('Test failed:', error);
        } else {
            console.log('Test successful');
        }
    });

    // Monitor real-time raw metrics data
    test.recieveOutput((data) => {
        console.log(`Processed ${data[0].length} requests`);
    });
}

runTest();
```

## 📊 Load Profiles

### 1. Fixed Load Profile

Maintains a constant number of virtual users throughout the test duration.

```javascript
const test = await new Perftest().create({
    collection: myCollection,
    durationMin: 5,
    vuCount: 100
});
```

### 2. Ramp-up Load Profile

Gradually increases virtual users from initial count to peak count.

```javascript
const rampUpProfile = {
    "type": "ramp-up",
    "shorthand": "Ramp up (20 seconds)",
    "description": "5 virtual users run for 10 seconds, ramp up to 20 for 20 seconds, then maintain 20 for 30 seconds, each executing all requests sequentially.",
    "options": {
        "totalDuration": {
            "millis": 20000
        }
    },
    "controlPoints": [
        {
            "id": "initial",
            "load": { "count": 5 },
            "time": { "millis": 0 }
        },
        {
            "id": "rampLeadStart",
            "load": { "count": 5 },
            "time": { "millis": 5000 }
        },
        {
            "id": "rampLeadEnd",
            "load": { "count": 20 },
            "time": { "millis": 10000 }
        },
        {
            "id": "final",
            "load": { "count": 20 },
            "time": { "millis": 20000 }
        }
    ]
};

const test = await new Perftest().create({
    collection: myCollection,
    loadProfile: rampUpProfile
});
```

### 3. Spike Load Profile

Quickly spikes to high load and then drops back down.

```javascript
const spikeProfile = {
    "type": "spike",
    "shorthand": "Spike",
    "description": "2 virtual users run for 14 seconds, spikes to 20 over 16 seconds, drops to 2 over 14 seconds, maintains 2 for 16 seconds, each executing all requests sequentially.",
    "options": {
        "totalDuration": {
            "millis": 30000
        }
    },
    "controlPoints": [
        {
            "id": "initial",
            "load": { "count": 2 },
            "time": { "millis": 0 }
        },
        {
            "id": "spikeStart",
            "load": { "count": 2 },
            "time": { "millis": 5000 }
        },
        {
            "id": "spikePeak",
            "load": { "count": 20 },
            "time": { "millis": 10000 }
        },
        {
            "id": "spikeEnd",
            "load": { "count": 2 },
            "time": { "millis": 20000 }
        },
        {
            "id": "final",
            "load": { "count": 2 },
            "time": { "millis": 30000 }
        }
    ]
};

const test = await new Perftest().create({
    collection: myCollection,
    loadProfile: spikeProfile
});
```

### 4. Peak Load Profile

Ramps up to peak load, maintains it, then ramps down.

```javascript
const peakProfile = {
    "type": "peak",
    "shorthand": "Peak",
    "description": "4 virtual users run for 11 seconds, ramp up to 20 over 9 seconds, maintains 20 for 12 seconds, decrease to 4 over 11 seconds, maintains 4 for 17 seconds, each executing all requests sequentially.",
    "options": {
        "totalDuration": {
            "millis": 20000
        }
    },
    "controlPoints": [
        {
            "id": "initial",
            "load": { "count": 4 },
            "time": { "millis": 0 }
        },
        {
            "id": "rampLeadStart",
            "load": { "count": 4 },
            "time": { "millis": 9000 }
        },
        {
            "id": "rampLeadEnd",
            "load": { "count": 20 },
            "time": { "millis": 14000 }
        },
        {
            "id": "rampDownStart",
            "load": { "count": 20 },
            "time": { "millis": 16000 }
        },
        {
            "id": "rampDownEnd",
            "load": { "count": 4 },
            "time": { "millis": 18000 }
        },
        {
            "id": "final",
            "load": { "count": 4 },
            "time": { "millis": 20000 }
        }
    ]
};

const test = await new Perftest().create({
    collection: myCollection,
    loadProfile: peakProfile
});
```

## 🛠️ Configuration Options

### Test Configuration

| Parameter | Type | Description | Default |
|-----------|------|-------------|---------|
| `collection` | Object | Postman collection JSON | Required |
| `environment` | Object | Postman environment JSON | Optional |
| `globals` | Object | Global variables | `{}` |
| `secrets` | Object | Secret variables configuration | Optional |
| `durationMin` | Number | Test duration in minutes | Required (if no loadProfile) |
| `vuCount` | Number | Number of virtual users | Required (if no loadProfile) |
| `loadProfile` | Object | Custom load profile | Optional |
| `runId` | String/Number | Unique test run identifier |
| `dataFileConfig` | Object | Data file configuration | Optional |
| `isProcessRequired` | Boolean | Enable process-based execution | `false` |
| `requestSelection` | Array | Specific requests to execute | Optional |

### Data File Configuration

```javascript
const dataFileConfig = {
    distributionType: 'random', // or 'sequential' || 'ordered'
    contents: [
        { "name": "user1", "email": "user1@example.com" },
        { "name": "user2", "email": "user2@example.com" }
    ]
};
```

## 🎛️ Test Control

### Pause and Resume

```javascript
// Pause the test on demand
test.pause();

// Resume after on demamd
test.resume();

// Stop the test
test.stop();
```

### Event Handling

```javascript
test.on('init', () => {
    console.log('Test initialized');
});

test.on('start', () => {
    console.log('Test started');
});


test.on('status', (status) => {
    console.log('Status changed:', status);
});
```



## 🔧 Advanced Usage

### Custom Load Profiles

Create your own load profile:

```javascript
const customProfile = {
    type: "custom",
    shorthand: "Custom Profile",
    description: "Custom load pattern",
    options: {
        totalDuration: {
            millis: 60000
        }
    },
    controlPoints: [
        {
            id: "start",
            load: { count: 1 },
            time: { millis: 0 }
        },
        {
            id: "middle", 
            load: { count: 50 },
            time: { millis: 30000 }
        },
        {
            id: "end",
            load: { count: 1 },
            time: { millis: 60000 }
        }
    ]
};

const test = await new Perftest().create({
    collection: myCollection,
    loadProfile: customProfile
});
```

### Using with Secrets

```javascript
const secretsConfig = {
    id: "vault-id",
    values: [
        {
            key: "api_key",
            value: "secret-api-key",
            type: "secret",
            enabled: true
        }
    ]
};

const test = await new Perftest().create({
    collection: myCollection,
    secrets: secretsConfig,
    // ... other options
});
```
